leadText: Turnback Cave (Japanese: 戻りの洞窟 Returning Cave) is a cave which is home to the Legendary Pokémon Giratina. In Sinnoh, it is located at the north-central region of Sendoff Spring, which itself may be accessed through the Spring Path. The cave can only be entered after entering the Hall of Fame. In Hisui, it is located in the center of a tall mountain crater in the Spring Path, which lies at the northwestern corner of the Cobalt Coastlands. Due to the slope of the crater, Turnback Cave is primarily accessible via Ride Pokémon. Escape Ropes and Dig cannot be used to leave Turnback Cave.

sections.title: In the games
sections.text: Contents 1 In the games 1.1 Geography 1.1.1 Pokémon Diamond, Pearl, Platinum, Brilliant Diamond, and Shining Pearl 1.1.1.1 Rooms and pillars 1.1.1.2 Giratina's Room 1.1.2 Pokémon Legends: Arceus 1.2 Items 1.3 Pokémon 1.3.1 Generation IV 1.3.1.1 Before encountering a pillar 1.3.1.2 Between first and second pillars 1.3.1.3 Between second and third pillars 1.3.1.3.1 Note: After third pillar 1.3.2 Generation VIII 1.3.2.1 Brilliant Diamond and Shining Pearl 1.3.2.1.1 Before encountering a pillar 1.3.2.1.2 Between first and second pillars 1.3.2.1.3 Between second and third pillars 1.4 Special encounters 1.4.1 Pokémon Diamond and Pearl 1.4.2 Pokémon Platinum 1.4.3 Pokémon Brilliant Diamond and Shining Pearl 1.4.4 Pokémon Legends: Arceus 1.5 Layout 1.5.1 Entrance, pillar room, and Giratina's room 1.5.2 Random rooms 1.6 Music In the games [ edit | edit source ] Geography [ edit | edit source ] Pokémon Diamond, Pearl, Platinum, Brilliant Diamond, and Shining Pearl [ edit | edit source ] Rooms and pillars [ edit | edit source ] A pillar Turnback Cave is a maze-like cave filled with dense fog . The cave has a fixed starting point—the entrance room—and a fixed terminal room—Giratina's room. The rooms between those points are random every time the player goes through a door, and the room generated will be in one of two types of layouts: a room with a single pillar in the center, or a room with an odd pattern of rocks that may obstruct other entrances. In Pokémon Diamond, Pearl , Brilliant Diamond, and Shining Pearl , the inscription on the wall on the central pedestal in the entrance room reads: ...Past three pillars... to the sleeping... ...before 30 is surpassed... In Pokémon Platinum , the inscription instead reads: ...Past three pillars... offer up...to the... ... before 30 is surpassed... To reach Giratina's room, the player must find three pillar rooms; the room immediately after the third pillar room found is guaranteed to be Giratina's room. If the player passes through a total of 30 rooms before arriving at Giratina's room, the next door that is entered will return the player to the entrance room, where another attempt can be made. Exiting a room through the door from which it was entered returns the player to the entrance room, with the exception of the third pillar room and the door from the entrance room returning to Sendoff Spring. Each other door has a 25% chance to lead to the next pillar room. In the pillar rooms, The inscription at the base of the pillars features two numbers. The first denotes which pillar room the player is in, while the second denotes how many total rooms the player has traversed (including the current room) since leaving the entrance room. In Pokémon Diamond, Pearl, and Platinum, saving the game and reloading from within any intermediate room will cause all doors in that room to lead back to the entrance room. In Pokémon Brilliant Diamond and Shining Pearl, reloading a save within an intermediate room will cause the door which the room was originally entered from to be forgotten, meaning none of the doors will lead to the entrance unless 30 rooms have already been entered. Giratina's Room [ edit | edit source ] Giratina's room has four entrances like other rooms in the cave, and from each a set of stairs descends to a central pedestal where Giratina stands (or an item is present if Giratina has already been caught). In Pokémon Platinum, Giratina is only present if the player did not catch it in the Distortion World ; if Giratina has been caught, there is a portal to the Distortion World where the player can obtain the Griseous Orb . If Giratina is not present, there is an item, which reappears upon each subsequent visit to the room. Exiting via any of the doors from this room returns the player to the entrance room. There is an inscription on the wall on the central pedestal that reads: This is... That where life sparkles... That where life has faded... A place where two worlds overlap... Pokémon Legends: Arceus [ edit | edit source ] Turnback Cave in Legends: Arceus Turnback Cave is a small yet spacious rounded cavern that is empty, with stone pillars lining the wall. Giratina will appear in the cave after accepting Request 91: "On The Trail of Giratina" from Professor Laventon . Items [ edit | edit source ] Item Location Games Reaper Cloth Giratina's room, if the player reached it by passing through only 3 rooms and Giratina is not present (reappears each time) D P Pt BD SP Rare Bone Giratina's room, if the player reached it by passing through 4-15 rooms and Giratina is not present (reappears each time) D P Pt BD SP Stardust Giratina's room, if the player reached it by passing through 16-30 rooms and Giratina is not present (reappears each time) D P Pt BD SP Griseous Core Rewarded for catching Giratina to complete Request 91 LA Pokémon [ edit | edit source ] Generation IV [ edit | edit source ] Before encountering a pillar [ edit | edit source ] Pokémon Games Location Levels Rate Golbat D P Pt Cave 45-46 30% Golbat D P Pt Cave 17-18 25% Gastly D P Pt Cave 15, 17 30% Gastly D P Pt Cave 16 10% 10% 0% Haunter D P Pt Cave 44-46 30% Duskull D P Pt Cave 18 5% Dusclops D P Pt Cave 16 0% 0% 10% Chingling D P Pt Cave 16, 18 10% Bronzor D P Pt Cave 44 5% Bronzor D P Pt Cave 15 20% Bronzong D P Pt Cave 45-46 35% Special Pokémon Lunatone D P Pt Dual-slot Sapphire 44, 46 8% Lunatone D P Pt Dual-slot Sapphire 18 8% Solrock D P Pt Dual-slot Ruby 44, 46 8% Solrock D P Pt Dual-slot Ruby 18 8% Dusclops D P Pt Dual-slot FRLG E 18 4% Dusclops D P Pt Poké Radar 18 1% A colored background means that the Pokémon can be found in this location in the specified game. A white background with a colored letter means that the Pokémon cannot be found here. Between first and second pillars [ edit | edit source ] Pokémon Games Location Levels Rate Golbat D P Pt Cave 55-56 30% Golbat D P Pt Cave 25, 27 25% Haunter D P Pt Cave 54-56 30% Haunter D P Pt Cave 25, 27 30% Haunter D P Pt Cave 26 10% 10% 0% Chimecho D P Pt Cave 28 1% Duskull D P Pt Cave 28 4% Dusclops D P Pt Cave 26 0% 0% 10% Dusclops D P Pt Cave 28 1% Chingling D P Pt Cave 26, 28 9% Bronzor D P Pt Cave 54 5% Bronzor D P Pt Cave 25 20% Bronzong D P Pt Cave 55-56 35% Special Pokémon Lunatone D P Pt Dual-slot Sapphire 54, 56 8% Lunatone D P Pt Dual-slot Sapphire 28 8% Solrock D P Pt Dual-slot Ruby 54, 56 8% Solrock D P Pt Dual-slot Ruby 28 8% Dusclops D P Pt Dual-slot FRLG E 28 4% A colored background means that the Pokémon can be found in this location in the specified game. A white background with a colored letter means that the Pokémon cannot be found here. Between second and third pillars [ edit | edit source ] Only five of the six possible rooms have wild encounters; the second of the possible rooms between these pillars never has wild encounters. Pokémon Games Location Levels Rate Golbat D P Pt Cave 65-66 30% Golbat D P Pt Cave 37-38 25% Haunter D P Pt Cave 64-66 30% Haunter D P Pt Cave 35, 37 30% Haunter D P Pt Cave 36 10% 10% 0% Dusclops D P Pt Cave 36 0% 0% 10% Dusclops D P Pt Cave 38 5% Chimecho D P Pt Cave 36, 38 10% Bronzor D P Pt Cave 64 5% Bronzong D P Pt Cave 65-66 35% Bronzong D P Pt Cave 35 20% Special Pokémon Lunatone D P Pt Dual-slot Sapphire 64, 66 8% Lunatone D P Pt Dual-slot Sapphire 38 8% Solrock D P Pt Dual-slot Ruby 64, 66 8% Solrock D P Pt Dual-slot Ruby 38 8% A colored background means that the Pokémon can be found in this location in the specified game. A white background with a colored letter means that the Pokémon cannot be found here. Note: After third pillar [ edit | edit source ] While there is a fourth encounter table in the game code for generating encounters where everything is a further 10 levels higher after the third pillar, it is not used and is thus not encounterable through vanilla gameplay. Any tables with values of a Lvl. 45 Bronzong in Platinum, for example, are not representative of vanilla gameplay. Generation VIII [ edit | edit source ] Brilliant Diamond and Shining Pearl [ edit | edit source ] Before encountering a pillar [ edit | edit source ] Pokémon Games Location Levels Rate Golbat BD SP Cave 45-46 30% Haunter BD SP Cave 44-46 30% Bronzor BD SP Cave 44 5% Bronzong BD SP Cave 45-46 35% A colored background means that the Pokémon can be found in this location in the specified game. A white background with a colored letter means that the Pokémon cannot be found here. A white background with a colored letter means that the Pokémon cannot be found here. Special encounters [ edit | edit source ] Pokémon Diamond and Pearl [ edit | edit source ] A Giratina appears here on the first visit. Force Ghost Physical Heal Block Psychic Status Earth Power Ground Special Slash Normal Physical Pokémon Platinum [ edit | edit source ] Altered Forme Giratina at Turnback Cave in Platinum An Altered Forme Giratina appears here after entering the Hall of Fame if it was defeated or run from in the Distortion World . If it is defeated or run from, it respawns after entering the Hall of Fame again. Giratina Lv. 47 Types: Ghost Dragon Ability: Pressure Held item: None Effort levels : Ominous Wind Ghost Special AncientPower Rock Special Dragon Claw Dragon Physical Shadow Force Ghost Physical Pokémon Brilliant Diamond and Shining Pearl [ edit | edit source ] An Altered Forme Giratina appears here on the first visit. If defeated or run from, it respawns after entering the Hall of Fame . Giratina Lv. 70 Types: Ghost Dragon Ability: Pressure Held item: None Effort levels : Shadow Force Ghost Physical Pain Split Normal Status Aura Sphere Fighting Special Dragon Claw Dragon Physical Pokémon Legends: Arceus [ edit | edit source ] Giratina in Pokémon Legends: Arceus Giratina can be caught in its Origin Forme during Request 91: "On the Trail of Giratina", which becomes available after completion of Mission 26: "Seeking the Remaining Plates". It is coded to never be Shiny . If defeated, it respawns after the player exits and reenters the cave.
